PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-15-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Carolyn Baulsier appeared by zoom for and with Christina Newman.

The Court asked about the damage to the will. Ms. Baulsier stated that she was Mr. Cardenas' family Attorney for over 20 years, that he never mentioned that he wanted to revoke or change the will, that the damage may have occurred by rodents, and that it was the original will and it didn't have any marks on it. Ms. Newman showed pictures of the condition of the house, stated that the will was in an original envelope with an original previous address of Ms. Baulsier, that it was on a bed where rodents were nesting, and that the rodents chewed through the envelope and three or four of the pages. The Court stated that it appears as is something was spilled on the will. Ms. Newman stated that she thinks the stains are rodent urine and that she had to move 100 cubic yards of garbage in order to find this will. Ms. Baulsier stated that Ms. Newman is the only heir of the estate, that if the will is not admitted it would be intestacy and that Ms. Newman would still be the heir. The Court stated that they have overcome the presumption of revocation and that there isn't any evidence that the Decedent wanted to revoke the will. The Court granted the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary. The will dated April 30, 2001 is admitted to probate. Christina Shelby Newman is appointed as the Executor of the estate with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for June 14, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
